question.text: For shares to be eligible as an Offer for Sale (OFS) in an IPO, the shares must have been held for at least how long before filing the draft offer document?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Regulation 8 of SEBI ICDR, shares offered for sale in an IPO must have been held for at least 1 year before the date of filing the draft offer document. Exceptions exist for government companies, statutory corporations, infrastructure SPVs, and shares acquired through court/tribunal-approved schemes (held >1 year). **The Trap:** 6 months is a common holding period in other contexts (e.g., pre-IPO placement lock-in), which can mislead candidates. The 2-year and 3-year options are fabricated distractors. **Cross-Exam Context:** Lock-in and holding period concepts are also tested in NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) relating to promoter lock-in post-IPO. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When advising promoters planning an OFS component in their IPO, ensure shareholding restructuring is completed well in advance — the 1-year clock starts from the date of acquisition, not from any board resolution.
